title: A Stranger in the Cold
description: You pass by a shivering figure huddled in a doorway. Their eyes meet yours—tired, hopeful, resigned. The winter wind bites at both of you.

choices.description: Give them your coat and buy them a warm meal.
choices.logMessage: A moment of kindness on a cold street left its mark on you.
choices.resultDialogText: The stranger's eyes light up with gratitude. You walk away colder but warmer inside.

choices.description: Avert your eyes and walk past quickly.
choices.logMessage: You walked on. The memory of that moment stayed with you.
choices.resultDialogText: You hurry on. The image of their face lingers longer than you expected.

choices.description: Call a shelter and wait with them until help arrives.
choices.logMessage: A small act of care on a cold night left its mark.
choices.resultDialogText: The shelter van arrives. You stay until they're gone, and something in you settles.

title: The Charity Drive
description: Your community is organizing a charity drive for families in need. Flyers are everywhere, and volunteers are signing up at the town hall.

choices.description: Lead the drive and recruit others to help.
choices.logMessage: Something in you and in those around you shifted that day.
choices.resultDialogText: The drive was a success. People still talk about how you brought the community together.

choices.description: Donate money anonymously and move on.
choices.logMessage: Your quiet generosity left a mark on you.
choices.resultDialogText: Your anonymous gift went out into the world. You felt lighter for it.

choices.description: Ignore it—you have your own problems.
choices.logMessage: A chance to reach out passed you by; you felt it afterward.
choices.resultDialogText: Life went on. But sometimes you wondered what might have been different.

title: The Injured Creature
description: You find a small animal—a bird or a cat—lying injured by the roadside. It's still breathing but clearly in pain. No one else is around.

choices.description: Carefully take it to a vet, paying for treatment.
choices.logMessage: A small life in your hands changed something in you.
choices.resultDialogText: The vet took the creature in. You left knowing it would be okay.

choices.description: Try to help it yourself with what you have.
choices.logMessage: You gave what you had. Sometimes that's enough.
choices.resultDialogText: You did what you could. It wasn't perfect, but it was enough.

choices.description: Leave it—nature will take its course.
choices.logMessage: You left it behind. The choice lingered.
choices.resultDialogText: You walked away. The image followed you for days.

title: Violence in the Alley
description: You round a corner and see two people—one is clearly the aggressor, the other is backed against a wall. You're the only witness.

choices.description: Intervene and try to stop the fight.
choices.logMessage: You stepped in. Not everyone would have.
choices.resultDialogText: The fight stopped. You took a few hits, but the victim got away.

choices.description: Call the authorities and stay to give a statement.
choices.logMessage: You did what you could from a distance—and it mattered.
choices.resultDialogText: Police arrived soon after. You gave your statement and left with a clearer conscience.

choices.description: Walk away—it's not your business.
choices.logMessage: You kept walking. The choice stayed with you.
choices.resultDialogText: You turned the corner. The sounds faded behind you.

title: The Stage Awaits
description: You've been asked to give a speech in front of a large audience. The room will be packed. Your palms are already sweating.

choices.description: Accept and prepare thoroughly—you'll face the fear.
choices.logMessage: You faced something that scared you—and grew from it.
choices.resultDialogText: You spoke. The room listened. When it was over, you felt different.

choices.description: Accept but wing it—confidence is key.
choices.logMessage: You took the stage without much preparation. It left its mark.
choices.resultDialogText: You got through it—barely. Next time, you might prepare a little more.

choices.description: Decline—you're not ready for that.
choices.logMessage: You turned it down. The opportunity passed, and something in you shifted.
choices.resultDialogText: You said no. Relief mixed with a quiet regret.

title: The Bully
description: Someone has been tormenting a classmate for weeks. Today they've cornered their victim again. Everyone is watching to see what happens.

choices.description: Confront the bully directly and defend the victim.
choices.logMessage: You stood up when it mattered. It cost you something.
choices.resultDialogText: The bully backed down. The victim thanked you later. You had a bruise, but you felt taller.

choices.description: Get a teacher—let authority handle it.
choices.logMessage: You found another way to help. It worked.
choices.resultDialogText: The teacher intervened. The situation de-escalated. You had done your part.

choices.description: Look away—you don't want to get involved.
choices.logMessage: You looked away. The moment stayed with you.
choices.resultDialogText: You pretended not to see. The guilt was quieter than the fear.

title: The Night Before
description: Your friends are throwing a party tonight. Your most important exam is tomorrow morning. The invitation is burning a hole in your pocket.

choices.description: Stay home and study—the exam matters more.
choices.logMessage: You chose the books over the party. It paid off.
choices.resultDialogText: You studied through the night. The exam went well. You felt tired but clear.

choices.description: Go for an hour, then come back to study.
choices.logMessage: You balanced both. It wasn't easy, but you managed.
choices.resultDialogText: An hour turned into two. You made it back and studied until dawn. You were exhausted but you tried.

choices.description: Forget the exam—you'll wing it. Party all night.
choices.logMessage: You chose the party. The morning after was rough.
choices.resultDialogText: You had fun. The exam was a blur. Your head hurt for days.

title: The Easy Way Out
description: A colleague suggests cutting corners on a project to meet the deadline. No one would notice, they say. The proper way would take twice as long.

choices.description: Refuse—do the job properly, even if it's late.
choices.logMessage: You chose the harder path. It shaped you.
choices.resultDialogText: You delivered it right, a few days late. Your boss noticed—and respected it.

choices.description: Take the shortcut—everyone does it sometimes.
choices.logMessage: You took the easy way. Something in you knew.
choices.resultDialogText: The project shipped on time. No one said anything. You wondered if they knew.

choices.description: Report the suggestion to your supervisor.
choices.logMessage: You spoke up when it mattered. It wasn't easy.
choices.resultDialogText: Your supervisor thanked you. The colleague was reassigned. The project was done right.

title: A Questionable Opportunity
description: Someone offers you a deal that would solve your money problems overnight. It's technically legal but morally gray. They need an answer soon.

choices.description: Decline—your principles aren't for sale.
choices.logMessage: You walked away from easy money. You slept better for it.
choices.resultDialogText: You said no. The offerer seemed surprised. You felt lighter.

choices.description: Take the deal—you need the money.
choices.logMessage: You took the deal. Your pockets were fuller; something else felt emptier.
choices.resultDialogText: The money arrived. You paid the bills. A small voice asked if it was worth it.

choices.description: Ask for time to think—then seek advice from someone you trust.
choices.logMessage: You paused and sought counsel. The choice became clearer.
choices.resultDialogText: You talked it through. In the end, you knew what to do—and you did it.

title: The Gray Area
description: You've discovered something that could hurt people if revealed—but keeping it secret could also cause harm. There's no clear right answer.

choices.description: Reveal the truth and accept the consequences.
choices.logMessage: You spoke the truth. It cost you, but you could live with it.
choices.resultDialogText: The truth came out. Some people were hurt. Some were grateful. You stood by your choice.

choices.description: Keep it secret—sometimes ignorance is kinder.
choices.logMessage: You held the secret. It weighed on you.
choices.resultDialogText: You said nothing. Life went on. The burden stayed with you.

choices.description: Seek counsel from someone wiser before deciding.
choices.logMessage: You sought wisdom before acting. It changed how you saw things.
choices.resultDialogText: Their advice helped. You made a decision you could stand by.

title: A Guiding Hand
description: Someone experienced has taken an interest in you. They offer to teach you what they know—but it will require dedication and humility to learn.

choices.description: Accept gratefully and commit to learning.
choices.logMessage: You opened yourself to learning. It changed your path.
choices.resultDialogText: The lessons began. You had so much to learn—and you were ready.

choices.description: Thank them but decline—you prefer to learn on your own.
choices.logMessage: You chose to go it alone. The road felt longer.
choices.resultDialogText: You thanked them and walked away. Independence had its price.

choices.description: Accept but only take what's useful—you know your path.
choices.logMessage: You took what served you. It was enough.
choices.resultDialogText: You learned selectively. Some of it stuck. You moved forward.

title: The Hard Truth
description: Something you believed about yourself or the world has been proven wrong. The realization is uncomfortable but undeniable.

choices.description: Embrace the lesson and grow from it.
choices.logMessage: The truth hurt—but you let it change you.
choices.resultDialogText: You accepted it. It wasn't easy, but you grew.

choices.description: Reject it—you're not ready to change.
choices.logMessage: You pushed it away. It pushed back.
choices.resultDialogText: You couldn't accept it. The weight of denial stayed with you.

choices.description: Share the lesson with others who might benefit.
choices.logMessage: You turned pain into something you could give away.
choices.resultDialogText: You shared what you'd learned. Someone thanked you. It helped.

title: The Open Road
description: A friend suggests dropping everything and taking a spontaneous trip. No plans, no itinerary—just adventure. You have responsibilities, but the idea is intoxicating.

choices.description: Say yes—life is too short for endless planning.
choices.logMessage: You said yes. The trip left its mark—on your spirit and your wallet.
choices.resultDialogText: You went. It was chaotic and wonderful. You came back tired, poorer, and glad you'd gone.

choices.description: Compromise—plan a shorter, structured trip together.
choices.logMessage: You found a middle ground. It worked for both of you.
choices.resultDialogText: You planned a weekend away. It was just enough adventure without the chaos.

choices.description: Decline—you have too much to do.
choices.logMessage: You stayed. Your responsibilities came first.
choices.resultDialogText: You said no. Your friend went without you. You wondered if you'd made the right call.

title: Show Your Talent
description: A local contest is accepting entries—art, writing, music, whatever you create. The prize isn't much, but the chance to share your work is real.

choices.description: Enter with something you've been working on.
choices.logMessage: You shared something you'd made. It felt like a leap.
choices.resultDialogText: You submitted your work. The judges took notice. You felt proud.

choices.description: Create something new just for this.
choices.logMessage: You pushed yourself to create something new. It showed.
choices.resultDialogText: You made something from scratch. It wasn't perfect, but it was yours.

choices.description: Skip it—you're not ready for that kind of exposure.
choices.logMessage: You held back. The chance passed.
choices.resultDialogText: You didn't enter. The contest came and went. You wondered what might have been.

title: The Surprise
description: Your friends have secretly planned a surprise party for you. You've just walked in. Balloons, cake, and familiar faces everywhere.

choices.description: Embrace it fully—dance, laugh, enjoy every moment.
choices.logMessage: You let yourself be celebrated. It filled you up.
choices.resultDialogText: You danced and laughed until late. You felt loved. You felt good.

choices.description: Thank everyone warmly but stay somewhat reserved.
choices.logMessage: You appreciated it, even if you held back a little.
choices.resultDialogText: You thanked everyone. You enjoyed it, in your own way. It was enough.

choices.description: Feel overwhelmed and need a moment alone.
choices.logMessage: The attention was too much. You stepped away.
choices.resultDialogText: You slipped outside for air. When you came back, they understood. It was okay.

title: In the Spotlight
description: You're being honored at a ceremony. People are applauding. Speeches are being made about your achievements. All eyes are on you.

choices.description: Accept graciously and thank those who helped you.
choices.logMessage: You accepted the honor with grace. It reflected well on you.
choices.resultDialogText: You thanked everyone who had helped. The applause felt earned.

choices.description: Bask in it—you earned this.
choices.logMessage: You soaked it in. Something in you swelled.
choices.resultDialogText: You stood in the spotlight and loved it. A few people noticed.

choices.description: Deflect credit to your team and mentors.
choices.logMessage: You shared the credit. People remembered that.
choices.resultDialogText: You named everyone who had helped. Your team appreciated it. So did the crowd.

title: When Things Fall Apart
description: Something you worked hard for has failed. A project, a relationship, a goal—it's over. The disappointment is heavy.

choices.description: Accept it, learn from it, and move forward.
choices.logMessage: You took the blow and got back up. It changed you.
choices.resultDialogText: It hurt. But you learned. You moved on, a little wiser.

choices.description: Blame others and refuse to accept responsibility.
choices.logMessage: You pointed fingers. The weight didn't lift.
choices.resultDialogText: You blamed everyone else. The failure stayed. So did the bitterness.

choices.description: Let it crush you for a while—you need to grieve.
choices.logMessage: You let yourself feel it. It took its toll.
choices.resultDialogText: You fell apart for a while. When you came back, you were different.

title: Whose Idea Was It?
description: A project you contributed to is getting praise. Your contribution was significant, but so were others'. The boss is asking who led the effort.

choices.description: Share credit equally with the team.
choices.logMessage: You shared the spotlight. People remembered.
choices.resultDialogText: You named everyone. The team appreciated it. Trust grew.

choices.description: Claim it was mostly your work.
choices.logMessage: You took the credit. Some relationships shifted.
choices.resultDialogText: You claimed the spotlight. A few colleagues exchanged looks. Things were different after that.

choices.description: Deflect to the person who had the original idea.
choices.logMessage: You gave credit where it was due. It mattered.
choices.resultDialogText: You pointed to them. They were surprised—and grateful. The gesture was noted.

title: Running on Empty
description: The year has been relentless. Your body and mind are sending signals—exhaustion, irritability, a sense that you cannot keep this pace.

choices.description: Take a step back and prioritize rest.
choices.logMessage: You listened to your body. It thanked you.
choices.resultDialogText: You slowed down. The exhaustion eased. You felt human again.

choices.description: Push through—you have too much at stake.
choices.logMessage: You pushed on. The cost was real.
choices.resultDialogText: You kept going. You made it—barely. Your body paid the price.

title: A Call from Home
description: Your parent has fallen ill. The doctor says it is serious but manageable with care. Your family is asking how much you can help.

choices.description: Make time to visit and help regularly.
choices.logMessage: You showed up when it mattered. It cost you, but you were there.
choices.resultDialogText: You visited often. They were grateful. You were tired, but you had no regrets.

choices.description: Send money but keep your distance.
choices.logMessage: You helped from afar. It was something—but not everything.
choices.resultDialogText: The check was sent. You stayed away. The guilt was quiet but present.

title: The Reorganization
description: Rumors have become reality: the company is restructuring. Your role might change, move, or disappear. Everyone is anxious.

choices.description: Stay calm and wait for the dust to settle.
choices.logMessage: You kept your head down. It paid off.
choices.resultDialogText: The reorganization came and went. Your role survived. You breathed easier.

choices.description: Start looking for other opportunities.
choices.logMessage: You took control. It felt empowering.
choices.resultDialogText: You updated your resume. Options opened up. You felt less trapped.

title: The Invitation
description: A friend is throwing a party and wants you there. It is the kind of night that could go anywhere—fun, chaos, or both.

choices.description: Go and enjoy yourself.
choices.logMessage: You went. It was worth it.
choices.resultDialogText: You had a great night. Your friend was glad you came. You felt recharged.

choices.description: Decline—you need a quiet night.
choices.logMessage: You chose rest. Your batteries recharged.
choices.resultDialogText: You stayed in. The quiet was exactly what you needed.

title: A Chance to Ease the Load
description: Your dedication has not gone unnoticed. A scholarship opportunity has opened up—partial funding in exchange for good standing and a short essay.

choices.description: Apply and give it your best shot.
choices.logMessage: You took the chance. It paid off.
choices.resultDialogText: You got it. The financial relief was real. Your hard work had been noticed.

choices.description: Pass—you do not have the time to apply.
choices.logMessage: You let the chance slip. You felt it afterward.
choices.resultDialogText: The deadline passed. You hadn't applied. The opportunity was gone.

title: The Big Offer
description: A well-known tech company has reached out. They have seen your work and want to talk. The name alone would open doors for years.

choices.description: Pursue the opportunity seriously.
choices.logMessage: You went for it. The payoff was real.
choices.resultDialogText: You aced the interviews. The offer came. Your career trajectory shifted.

choices.description: Stay where you are—stability matters more.
choices.logMessage: You chose stability. You slept better for it.
choices.resultDialogText: You declined. Your current job felt safer. You wondered sometimes, but you didn't regret it.

title: The Case Everyone Is Watching
description: A high-profile case has landed on your desk. The media will follow every move. Win or lose, your name will be in the spotlight.

choices.description: Take the case and give it everything.
choices.logMessage: You took the risk. It defined your year.
choices.resultDialogText: The case consumed you. You won. Your name was in the papers. You were exhausted but proud.

choices.description: Pass to a senior colleague.
choices.logMessage: You knew your limits. It was the right call.
choices.resultDialogText: You handed it off. They handled it. You avoided the spotlight—and the stress.

title: The Code
description: A patient is crashing. You have minutes to decide the next move. The team is looking at you.

choices.description: Stay calm and follow protocol.
choices.logMessage: You kept your head. It saved a life.
choices.resultDialogText: You followed the steps. The patient stabilized. The team breathed again.

choices.description: Take a calculated risk to save them.
choices.logMessage: You took a chance. It worked.
choices.resultDialogText: You went off-protocol. The patient made it. Your heart was still racing.

title: An Editor's Interest
description: An editor has read your work and wants to discuss a potential project. It could be the break you have been waiting for.

choices.description: Meet and pitch your best idea.
choices.logMessage: You put yourself out there. It paid off.
choices.resultDialogText: The meeting went well. They wanted to work with you. A door had opened.

choices.description: Politely decline—you are not ready.
choices.logMessage: You stepped back. The chance passed.
choices.resultDialogText: You said you weren't ready. They understood. You wondered if you ever would be.

title: 48 Hours to Build
description: Your department is putting together a team for a hackathon. It is intense, collaborative, and could lead to real opportunities.

choices.description: Join the team and give it your all.
choices.logMessage: You threw yourself into it. The weekend changed you.
choices.resultDialogText: Forty-eight hours of caffeine and code. You didn't win, but you learned. You were exhausted.

choices.description: Skip it—you need to focus on coursework.
choices.logMessage: You chose the books. You wondered what you'd missed.
choices.resultDialogText: You stayed in and studied. Your grades were solid. You heard the hackathon was intense.

title: First Real Responsibility
description: On your clinical rotation, a patient's family is asking you to do something that conflicts with what the attending said. You are caught in the middle.

choices.description: Escalate to the attending and document everything.
choices.logMessage: You followed the rules. It was the right call.
choices.resultDialogText: The attending handled it. Your documentation protected everyone. You learned what integrity looked like in practice.

choices.description: Try to satisfy the family without going over the attending's head.
choices.logMessage: You tried to please everyone. It was exhausting.
choices.resultDialogText: You walked a tightrope. The family was somewhat appeased. The attending was not fully pleased. You were drained.

title: The Near Miss
description: During a site visit, you notice something that does not look right. Fixing it would delay the project and cost money. Ignoring it might be fine—or not.

choices.description: Report it and insist on a proper fix.
choices.logMessage: You spoke up. It wasn't popular, but it was right.
choices.resultDialogText: The fix was ordered. The project was delayed. No one got hurt. You slept well.

choices.description: Mention it informally and hope it gets addressed.
choices.logMessage: You said something—but not enough. It nagged at you.
choices.resultDialogText: You dropped a hint. You weren't sure anyone listened. The project continued.
